title: Developmental cell programs are co-opted in inflammatory skin disease
snippets: Two Schwann cell populations are identified in adult skin: myelinating (Schwaan_1) and non-myelinating (Schwaan_2), as part of the stromal cell compartment

title: Single-cell atlas of peripheral nervous system cells reveals Schwann cell heterogeneity in human skin
snippets: Myelinating Schwann cells are characterised by high expression of MPZ, MBP and PLP1, which encode the major structural proteins of compact peripheral myelin
Two Schwann cell subtypes exist in adult human skin: myelinating (MPZ+/MBP+) and non-myelinating (NGFR+/GFAP+/GLUL+)

title: Molecular aspects of peripheral myelination and its regulation
snippets: MPZ (P0) is the most abundant protein in peripheral nervous system (PNS) myelin, comprising over 50% of total PNS myelin protein, and is exclusively expressed in myelinating Schwann cells
